frameworks.name: prospective value computation
frameworks.one_line: Decisions valued by anticipated future states and own future actions

frameworks.name: model-based sequential decision making
frameworks.one_line: Planning over state transitions where benefits arrive only after later choices

frameworks.name: metacognitive self-modeling
frameworks.one_line: Agents reason about their own anticipated future biases when planning

core_question: How do humans plan sequential decisions whose payoffs depend on their own future behavior, and how is this prospective value represented in medial frontal cortex?
key_assumptions: Choices have delayed, contingent benefits requiring forward simulation
Agents can model their own future behavioral tendencies
Medial frontal cortex supports value-based prospective computation
manipulations: Sequential decision-making task with delayed contingent benefits
Behavioral testing in humans (n=76) and fMRI subsample (n=24)
key_findings: Participants computed prospective value taking future states and own responses into account
Participants anticipated their own future biases and avoided states triggering them
Dorsal anterior cingulate cortex tracked prospective value
Area 8m/9 reflected anticipated changes in prospective value; perigenual ACC reflected its influence on choice
interpretations: Prospective decision making is decomposable into dissociable medial frontal subcomponents
Insight into one's own future behavioral biases shapes adaptive sequential choice
